daily_brief: NATO leaders convene in Ankara today amid sharp tensions over burden-sharing and Ukraine support, with President Trump again questioning the alliance's value while European members prepare to announce €70 billion in military aid for Kyiv [BBC], [RFI]. The summit follows devastating Russian missile strikes on Kyiv that killed at least 28 civilians, exposing critical gaps in Ukraine's air defense capabilities as Patriot interceptor stocks run low [Globe and Mail], [BBC]. President Zelensky will press allies for urgent air defense systems and hold crucial talks with Trump on pathways to peace negotiations. In France, Marine Le Pen awaits a Paris appeal court verdict this afternoon that will determine whether she can pursue a fourth presidential bid; a conviction would hand the far-right National Rally candidacy to Jordan Bardella [BBC], [RFI]. Security concerns marked President Macron's historic visit to Syria as explosions struck near his Damascus hotel, injuring 18 people though the French leader was unharmed [BBC], [Al Jazeera], [Globe and Mail]. Meanwhile, a Qatari LNG tanker was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, with Iranian state media implying Tehran's involvement after the vessel allegedly ignored warnings [Financial Times], [The Hindu].

top_stories.headline: NATO Summit Opens in Ankara Amid Transatlantic Tensions Over Ukraine Support and Burden-Sharing
bullets: NATO leaders gather in Ankara for a two-day summit, with European members and Canada preparing to announce €70 billion in military aid commitments to Ukraine for 2026-2027

President Trump renewed criticism of NATO, calling the alliance 'ridiculous' and 'not reciprocal,' while Secretary General Mark Rutte emphasized the need for continued U.S. engagement alongside greater European defense spending
President Zelensky will hold direct talks with Trump to advocate for increased air defense systems and push for Russia to be pressured into peace negotiations

The summit follows intense Russian strikes on Ukraine and Ukrainian drone counterattacks that have caused fuel shortages in Russia, with Moscow reporting 430 drones intercepted overnight targeting the capital

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes Zelensky's appeal for air defenses and the humanitarian toll of Russian strikes

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Highlights transatlantic tensions and Trump's critical comments about NATO

sources.name: The Economist
sources.framing_note: Focuses on diplomatic strategies including 'Trump flattery' to preserve alliance unity

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Notes European military ramp-up is 'scattered and unsynchronized'

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Frames Russia as 'touting war with NATO' amid battlefield challenges

top_stories.headline: Russian Missile Barrage Kills at Least 28 in Kyiv, Exposing Critical Air Defense Gaps
bullets: Russian missiles and drones struck Kyiv and surrounding areas overnight Sunday-Monday, killing at least 28 people and damaging residential high-rises
Ukraine's military was unable to intercept any of the 23 ballistic missiles fired, reflecting depleted stocks of Patriot interceptors—the only system capable of downing ballistic projectiles
President Zelensky called the situation 'absurd' and requested U.S. licenses to domestically manufacture Patriot systems
The attack came days after another deadly assault on the capital and on the eve of the NATO summit where air defense will be a central agenda item

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Details civilian casualties and Zelensky's appeals ahead of NATO summit

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes the technical vulnerability due to Patriot shortages and Zelensky's production proposals

top_stories.headline: Marine Le Pen Awaits Appeal Verdict That Will Determine Her Presidential Candidacy
bullets: A Paris appeal court will rule at 13:30 local time on whether to uphold Le Pen's March 2025 conviction for embezzling €1.4 million in European Parliament funds
The original verdict imposed a five-year ban on holding public office, which would bar her from the April 2027 presidential election where she currently leads polls
If the ban is upheld, her protégé Jordan Bardella, 30, would become the National Rally's presidential candidate
Le Pen has denied organizing the fake jobs scheme but admitted to 'a mistake'; she plans to announce her intentions on TF1 television the same day as the verdict

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provides comprehensive background on the case and its political implications

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Profiles both Le Pen and Bardella as potential candidates

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Details the legal proceedings and possible scenarios including acquittal or continued appeal

top_stories.headline: Explosions Strike Damascus During Macron's Historic Visit to Post-Assad Syria
bullets: Two explosive devices detonated approximately 125 meters from the Four Seasons hotel where President Macron was meeting civil society groups, injuring 18 people including four police officers
Macron did not hear the explosions and proceeded to meet Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa at the presidential palace as scheduled
Macron is the first EU leader to visit Syria since rebels led by al-Sharaa toppled Bashar al-Assad's 24-year regime in 2024
The French president will travel to Ankara for the NATO summit; his office confirmed the visit 'continues as planned'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes security concerns and provides BBC Verify analysis of explosion location

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief video report focusing on the moment of explosion

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Stresses the security challenges facing post-Assad Syria

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Headline focused on explosions during Macron visit

top_stories.headline: Tanker Attacked in Strait of Hormuz as Iran Mourns Khamenei
bullets: A Qatari LNG tanker caught fire after being struck by a projectile near Limah, Oman, while traveling south through the Strait of Hormuz
Iranian state television reported the tanker was attacked after 'ignoring warnings,' implying Iranian responsibility, though no official claim was made
The UK Maritime Trade Operations centre confirmed the strike hit the vessel's port side; no environmental impact was reported
The attack comes amid ongoing funeral ceremonies for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, killed in a February 28 US-Israeli strike

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline-only coverage noting tanker hit in strait

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Factual report noting Iran suspected but no claim made

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Includes Iranian state media statement implying Tehran's involvement and context of Khamenei funeral

additional_stories.headline: Belgium defeat USA 4-1 to reach World Cup quarter-finals after controversial FIFA decision allowed banned striker Balogun to play following Trump intervention
additional_stories.source: RFI English, Al Jazeera, Le Monde

additional_stories.headline: Severe storms including rare tornadoes kill at least 15 people and injure 275 in China's Hubei and Guangxi provinces, prompting President Xi to call for 'all out' rescue efforts
Indonesia and India sign missile supply deals for BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles and Astra air-to-air missiles during PM Modi's state visit to Jakarta
BBC investigation identifies jailers and officials accused of torture and sexual violence at Russia's secret detention facilities in occupied Ukraine
Microsoft to cut 4,800 jobs as company 'resets' its Xbox gaming division
Trump's immigration crackdown causing US companies to relocate jobs abroad as H-1B visa restrictions and $100,000 application fees drive employers to Canada, Mexico and India
Canada selects Germany's TKMS over South Korea's Hanwha for submarine fleet contract, with NATO interoperability cited as deciding factor
Landslides triggered by monsoon rains kill at least eight Rohingya refugees including five children in Bangladesh's Cox's Bazar camps
European Commission warns France that proposed under-15 social media ban would breach EU Digital Services Act
Liberia charges five suspects in $19 million cocaine trafficking bust after 200kg found disguised as Maggi seasoning cubes at Monrovia airport
